intended_use: MRIMath i2contour is intended for the semi-automatic labeling, visualization, and volumetric quantification of WHO grade 4 glioblastoma (GBM) from a set of standard MRI images of male or female patien
intended_use_full: MRIMath i2contour is intended for the semi-automatic labeling, visualization, and volumetric quantification of WHO grade 4 glioblastoma (GBM) from a set of standard MRI images of male or female patients 18 years of age or older who are known to have pathologically proven glioblastoma. Volumetric measurements may be compared to past measurements if available. MRIMath i2contour is not to be used for primary diagnosis and is not intended to be the sole diagnostic metric.
ground_truth: Manual segmentations by three US board-certified neuroradiologists with expertise in measuring glioblastoma, performed using the MRIMath smart manual contouring platform
other_metrics: Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC): T1c mean 0.95 (95% CI 93%-96%), FLAIR mean 0.92 (95% CI 90%-94%); T1c true positive DSC 83% vs radiologists 76%-86%; FLAIR true positive DSC 80% vs radiologists 75%-83%; Hausdorff distances < 5 mm for both; kappa scores reported; Bland-Altman differences reported; inter-user variability < 5% (T1c) and < 10% (FLAIR); 85% of FLAIR DSC measurements and 93% of T1c DSC measurements exceeded predicate device threshold of 0.88
